name: Colomera and Iznalloz — circular Sierra del Pozuelo
name_original: Colomera y Iznalloz — circular Sierra del Pozuelo
region_intro: Road cycling around Colomera offers diverse terrain, from narrow valleys to hills and olive groves. The region features predominantly paved surfaces, making it suitable for road cyclists of varying fitness levels. Routes often traverse scenic areas, including the Embalse de Colomera (Colomera Reservoir) and provide views of the distant Sierra Nevada and Sierra Elvira mountain ranges. Elevated points offer panoramic vistas of the reservoir and surrounding villages. Best road cycling routes around Colomera The most popular road cycling route is Colomera and Iznalloz — circular Sierra del Pozuelo, a moderate 39.7 miles (63.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Enmedio y Moclín - circular from Pinos Puente, a moderate 33.2 miles (53.5 km) path. This route offers views of the Sierra de Enmedio and passes near historic sites. Local road cyclists also love the View of Moclín Castle – Puerto de Moclín loop from Moclín, a 27.5 miles (44.2 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Colomera is defined by narrow valleys, hills, and olive groves, with routes offering views of the Embalse de Colomera and distant mountain ranges. The network offers options for all ability levels, from easy rides to challenging excursions with significant climbing. The routes in Colomera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Colomera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Colomera?
A: Road cycling around Colomera features diverse terrain, including narrow valleys, rolling hills, and expansive olive groves. The routes are predominantly on paved surfaces, offering a smooth experience for road cyclists. You'll encounter varied landscapes and elevation changes, with some routes providing significant climbing.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Colomera?
A: Yes, Colomera offers options for all ability levels. While many routes involve significant elevation, there are easier rides available, some lasting up to two hours with minimal elevation gain, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Colomera?
A: Experienced cyclists seeking a challenge will find several difficult routes around Colomera. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32141645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Moclín Castle – Puerto de Moclín loop from Moclín&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 27.5-mile (44.2 km) trail with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19440054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascante Tower loop from Tózar&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 82 kilometers with significant climbing.

Q: What scenic views and landmarks can I expect to see while cycling in Colomera?
A: Colomera's road cycling routes offer spectacular views of the Embalse de Colomera (Colomera Reservoir) and the surrounding village, especially from elevated points like Puerto de los Lobos Harteros. You can also enjoy panoramic vistas of the distant Sierra Nevada and Sierra Elvira mountain ranges. Many routes pass by historical sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5277382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moclín Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Colomera area?
A: Yes, Colomera features several excellent circular road cycling routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983606332/colomera-and-iznalloz-circular-sierra-del-pozuelo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomera and Iznalloz — circular Sierra del Pozuelo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 39.7-mile (63.8 km) trail. Another local favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983603710/sierra-de-enmedio-y-moclin-circular-from-pinos-puente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Enmedio y Moclín - circular from Pinos Puente&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the Sierra de Enmedio.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Colomera?
A: The road cycling routes in Colomera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning views of the reservoir and distant mountains, and the opportunity to combine challenging rides with visits to historical landmarks.

Q: Can I find places to stop for refreshments or food along the cycling routes?
A: While specific cafe stops are not detailed for every route, Colomera and nearby villages offer amenities. The municipality is also developing a large recreation area near the Embalse de Colomera, which will include restaurants and tourist services, providing convenient stops for cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Colomera?
A: Colomera's climate generally allows for road cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Even in winter, many routes remain accessible, though it's always advisable to check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any historical sites or natural attractions near the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes pass by or offer views of significant historical and natural attractions. You can cycle near the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5277382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moclín Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a former Nasrid kingdom frontier. The region also features natural caves like Cueva de Ollafría, a Roman bridge, and a Visigothic necropolis, adding cultural interest to your ride.

Q: Is public transport available to access road cycling routes in Colomera?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads are not detailed, Colomera is a town in the province of Granada. It's recommended to check local bus schedules or consider driving to a suitable starting point, especially for routes that begin outside the immediate town center.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest related to water bodies along the routes?
A: Yes, the Embalse de Colomera (Colomera Reservoir) is a prominent feature, offering scenic backdrops for many cycling routes. You can also find routes that traverse the riverbank of the Velillos River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another notable water body in the broader region that might be visible or accessible from some routes.
tips: The Colomera Reservoir is located in a geographical enclave in the middle of the junction of two rivers. The first, the Colomera River that comes from the Sierra Alta de Coloma and the second, the Río de las Juntas, coming from the Sierra de Lucena, both located to the north of the swamp.

Iznalloz is a town located above the Tajo de la Hoz, followed by the Río Cubilles. The Sierra de Arana (which reaches more than two thousand meters), forms a southern wall where a valley descends that reaches the city of Granada.
Roman construction, approximately between the 1st and 2nd centuries BC. C., whose mission was to contain the waters of the Cubillas River.
